page: Amazon Web Services m8i.48xlarge is a general-purpose server instance powered by the Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it features 192 virtual CPUs mapped to 96 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled. The processor operates at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz and includes a 480 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 768.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 7 GHz, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance operates on the Nitro hypervisor and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 75 Gbps. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier multi-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core CPU metrics and Geekbench multi-core scores placing in the top 10% of tested systems. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with read-only large block speeds reaching 585.77 GB/sec and write speeds at 241.13 GB/sec, both ranking in the top 10%. However, single-core performance presents a relative tradeoff; while the Geekbench single-core score ranks in the top 10%, the stress-ng single-core CPU performance falls into the middle 50% average category. The processor supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX), which accelerate workloads like cryptography and matrix operations.

This instance is designed for large-scale enterprise workloads that require high parallel processing capabilities and substantial memory bandwidth without the need for local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration. Top-tier performance in Redis SET operations (273.77 million operations per second) and static web serving throughput (270.13 GB/sec) makes it suitable for high-throughput database hosting, web serving, and software compilation. Additionally, its strong performance in LLM inference prompt processing across small, medium, and large models supports CPU-based machine learning tasks, offering a resource-dense alternative to GPU-accelerated instances for specific inference workloads.
description: Amazon Web Services m8i.48xlarge is a general-purpose server featuring 192 vCPUs and 96 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz. It is equipped with 768.0 GB of DDR5 memory and a baseline network bandwidth of 75 Gbps, though it lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance delivers top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance, ranking in the top 10% in multi-threaded benchmarks and Redis operations. A key tradeoff is its single-core performance, which ranges from average to top-tier depending on the workload. Supported by Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets, it is optimized for high-throughput database operations, software compilation, static web serving, and CPU-based LLM inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8i.48xlarge is a general-purpose server with 192 vCPUs, 768 GB DDR5 RAM, and 75 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core performance for enterprise databases and web serving.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8i.48xlarge is a general-purpose server featuring 192 vCPUs, 768 GB DDR5 RAM, and 75 Gbps network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor.
tagline: A high-capacity x86_64 platform with 192 vCPUs and 768 GB DDR5 memory, optimized for memory-intensive multi-threaded enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 192 vCPUs and 96 physical Intel Xeon cores running at 3.9 GHz
768.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory
75 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Top-tier multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and Redis performance
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated matrix and cryptographic operations
Suited for high-throughput databases, software compilation, and CPU-based LLM inference
